event: implantation of a biventricular pacing implantable cardioverter defibrillator
transported to our hospital by ambulance
Japan Coma Scale (JCS) score was II-10
no clear neurological deficits were observed
hemorrhage in the left lateral ventricle
hemorrhage in the right lateral ventricle
hemorrhage was confined within the ventricles
source of bleeding was unclear in the arterial phase
spot enhancement on the lateral wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
cerebral angiograph revealed an aneurysm at a distal site of the mLSA arising from the A1 segment of the left ACA
guiding sheath was navigated into the petrous portion of the left internal carotid artery
catheter was advanced to the left A1
catheterization was difficult due to the mLSA arising in a direction reverse to the path of the left A1
compression study of the right common carotid artery confirmed the anterior communicating artery
guiding sheath was introduced into the petrous portion of the right internal carotid artery
catheter was advanced through the AComA into the mLSA origin but was unable to be advanced to a site near the aneurysm
mLSA cannulation was possible after changing the catheter and guide wire
selective angiography confirmed the location of the catheter tip proximal to the aneurysm
perforating artery distal and proximal to the aneurysm were confirmed to be filled with NBCA
angiography after NBCA injection demonstrated disappearance of the aneurysm
slight reflux of NBCA was observed in the proximal artery
postoperative head CT showed no hemorrhagic complications
no development of clear cerebral infarction on postoperative CT
neither impaired consciousness nor paralysis developed
Lipiodol remained as a high-density spot on the lateral ventricular wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
decrease in muscle strength associated with disuse remained
